objective: Unblock the governance Overlord Sweep self-learning report step from GitHub-hosted runner failures caused by missing operator-local Claude memory files, while preserving strict local memory integrity validation.
scope_boundary: Modify only the governance memory integrity audit, its sweep invocation, focused tests, and issue-backed fail-fast/error-pattern planning evidence.
Preserve strict default local behavior for operator memory checks.
Use issue #481 as the governing issue for the runner-specific blocker discovered while proving issue #475.
out_of_scope: Changing the self-learning report implementation beyond validating it still builds.
Committing operator-local Claude memory files or secrets.
Redesigning the memory writer or long-term memory source of truth.
research_summary: Manual sweep run 24738583207 checked out all governed repositories and passed model-pin validation, then failed at Memory integrity audit because scripts/overlord/memory_integrity.py expected local Claude memory paths under /home/runner/.claude/projects. That skipped Build self-learning knowledge report. A supervised Claude worker independently identified the same runner-local path assumption and recommended an explicit CI skip with visible logging.

sprints.name: Runner-safe memory integrity gate
sprints.goal: Let GitHub-hosted sweep runs continue to self-learning when operator-home memory files are unavailable, without weakening local strict validation.
tasks: Add a memory_integrity.py option that treats missing memory files as explicit SKIP notices.
Keep malformed existing memory files as hard failures.
Run the sweep memory audit with the explicit runner mode.
Add focused unit tests for strict, skip, valid, and malformed paths.
Record the failure in fail-fast and error-pattern docs.
acceptance_criteria: Strict local memory integrity remains the default behavior.
Runner-mode missing memory exits 0 with SKIP notices.
Malformed existing memory files fail even in runner mode.
The Overlord Sweep reaches Build self-learning knowledge report after merge.
Validation evidence records any remaining gap before closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Inspect the runner failure and recommend the smallest change that preserves local strict behavior.
specialist_reviews.summary: The worker identified the hardcoded operator-home Claude memory path as the runner blocker and recommended an explicit, visible CI skip path for missing local memory.

alternate_model_review.summary: This is a deterministic runner-gate bugfix with focused tests and no standards or architecture policy change.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #481 may update the memory integrity audit, sweep invocation, focused tests, fail-fast/error-pattern docs, plan, scope, and validation evidence required to unblock the self-learning report step.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Publish the scoped fix, merge after CI, and re-run the Overlord Sweep to verify self-learning report execution.

material_deviation_rules: If the runner-mode audit hides malformed existing memory, stop and harden the distinction before publishing.
If the next sweep still skips self-learning, create or update an issue for the newly failed step before claiming closure.
If local strict memory validation fails, do not proceed until the local memory source is repaired or a separate issue-backed deferral is created.
